summary: As of Jan 5, 2026: D: 213, R: 219, Vacant: 3. MTG resigned effective Jan 5. Six-seat Republican advantage.
content:
Full House composition timeline from Wikipedia (119th Congress):
- Jan 20, 2025: D: 215, R: 218, V: 2 (question baseline)
- April 2, 2025: D: 214, R: 220, V: 2 (FL-1 and FL-6 specials, both R holds)
- Current as of Jan 5, 2026: D: 213, R: 219, V: 3

Three current vacancies:
1. TX-18 (D seat) - Turner died Mar 5 2025. Runoff Jan 31, 2026 (expected D)
2. NJ-11 (D seat) - Sherrill resigned Nov 20 2025 (elected NJ Gov). Special Apr 16, 2026 (likely D)
3. GA-14 (R seat) - MTG resigned Jan 5, 2026. Special TBD.

If all vacancies filled by same party: D: 215, R: 220 → 5-seat R advantage.
Democrats need D > R, so they'd need massive swing through special elections only.
No seats have flipped parties in the 119th Congress so far.
2026 midterm results don't take effect until Jan 3, 2027 (the deadline).
